narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout
narratives.thesis: Demand for AI chips and related infrastructure remains robust, driving strong performance in key semiconductor companies and their suppliers.
watchFor: Nvidia earnings miss or weak guidance
Hyperscaler capex cuts at next earnings
Significant slowdown in enterprise AI adoption

narratives.name: GLP-1 Obesity Wave
narratives.thesis: The market for GLP-1 obesity and diabetes drugs continues to expand rapidly, with leading manufacturers benefiting from strong demand and positive clinical trial results.

Significant side effect concerns emerge for GLP-1 drugs
Insurance coverage for GLP-1s becomes more restrictive

narratives.name: Semiconductor Equipment Expansion
narratives.thesis: The ongoing buildout of AI infrastructure and advanced manufacturing capacity is fueling strong demand for semiconductor equipment and materials.
watchFor: Major chipmakers announce production cuts
Geopolitical tensions disrupt global supply chains
ASML order book declines significantly

narratives.name: AI Software & Cloud Adoption
narratives.thesis: Enterprises are increasingly adopting AI-powered software and cloud solutions, driving growth for companies providing these services and platforms.

Enterprise IT spending surveys show AI budget cuts

narratives.name: China AI Chip Export Controls
narratives.thesis: US export controls on advanced AI chips are forcing companies to concede market share in China, creating headwinds for those reliant on that market.

Chinese domestic chipmakers fail to innovate rapidly
Companies find effective workarounds for export restrictions

narratives.thesis: The increasing adoption of AI across enterprises is driving demand for specialized hardware, leading to positive analyst sentiment and price target hikes for key players.

Competition intensifies, leading to price compression
Supply chain issues constrain hardware availability

Increased competition from new entrants or regional players

Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant theme, with strong demand for chips, equipment, and related software. Government initiatives in quantum computing are adding another layer of bullish sentiment, though China export controls remain a potential headwind.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Nvidia earnings miss or weak guidance — would signal a slowdown in AI chip demand
Expansion of China export controls to more advanced AI components — could impact supply chains and revenue

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: While some segments like auto manufacturers are seeing tailwinds from specific product launches, overall consumer spending is showing signs of pressure due to high gas prices and inflation, impacting retail sectors.
watchFor: Core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — would signal persistent inflation hurting discretionary spending
Consumer confidence index drops significantly — indicates weakening consumer sentiment
Retail sales data shows broad-based decline — confirms consumer spending slowdown

Communication Services.thesis: While digital advertising and content platforms continue to show strength, geopolitical tensions are creating headwinds for some entertainment companies.
watchFor: Global advertising spending growth slows significantly — impacts internet content and ad agencies
Major streaming service subscriber growth stalls — signals saturation
Escalation of geopolitical conflicts — further impacts international entertainment revenue

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense continues to see steady demand, while heavy construction machinery is showing resilience. Logistics is stable, but overall industrial sentiment is influenced by broader economic factors and specific company performance.
watchFor: Significant cuts to defense budgets — would impact aerospace & defense
Global trade tensions escalate — could hurt integrated freight & logistics
Manufacturing PMI drops below 50 for several months — signals industrial slowdown

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive strong performance for leading manufacturers, but the broader healthcare sector faces pressures from investor exits and mixed sentiment in medical devices.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy/safety — could challenge leaders
Increased regulatory scrutiny on GLP-1 pricing — impacts drug manufacturers
Major healthcare policy changes in the US — could affect healthcare plans

Financials.thesis: While some banks and asset managers are showing resilience and positive analyst sentiment, broader market concerns about junk debt and potential interest rate hikes create a cautious outlook for the sector.
watchFor: Federal Reserve signals rate cuts — would boost duration-sensitive assets
Significant increase in corporate defaults — impacts credit services and banks
SEC introduces new regulations impacting capital markets — could increase compliance costs

Consumer Defensive.thesis: Discount stores are demonstrating resilience as consumers seek value amidst high gas prices, but overall sentiment is cautious due to broader inflationary pressures.
watchFor: Significant decline in inflation (CPI < 0.2% MoM) — would reduce demand for discount stores
Consumer spending shifts back to discretionary items — hurts defensive retail
Major retailers announce aggressive price cuts — increases competition

Utilities.thesis: The utilities sector, particularly renewable energy and independent power producers, is benefiting from strong demand driven by data center expansion and climate change initiatives.
watchFor: Significant increase in interest rates — makes utility dividends less attractive
Slowdown in data center construction — impacts power demand
Policy shifts away from renewable energy incentives — hurts renewable utilities

Basic Materials.thesis: The rare earth materials sector is seeing a rebound due to US investment in critical technologies and a lack of significant policy changes in US-China relations, suggesting continued demand.
watchFor: Significant de-escalation of US-China trade tensions — could reduce strategic demand
New discoveries of rare earth deposits outside of China — impacts supply dynamics
Technological advancements reduce reliance on rare earth elements

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold and silver are acting as safe-haven assets and inflation hedges amidst rising geopolitical tensions and persistent inflation, driving demand.
watchFor: Core CPI print < 0.2% MoM — would reduce inflation hedge demand
Significant de-escalation of geopolitical conflicts — diminishes safe-haven appeal
US Dollar Index (DXY) strengthens significantly — makes metals more expensive for foreign buyers
